description:
You are an IT-support technician who just received a ticket saying 
    “The new development VM is missing the configuration for the internal service
    ‘simpleservice’.  Please put the service back in place and leave clear evidence
    that it was done.”

    What you must achieve
    ---------------------
    1.  Create the following directory hierarchy (any missing parent folders must
        also be created):

            /home/user/simpleservice/logs

    2.  Create the service-side configuration file **/home/user/etc/simpleservice.conf**
        with exactly (byte-for-byte) the following eight lines, including the
        trailing newline at the end of the last line:

            [General]
            port = 5500
            mode = production

            [Logging]
            level = info
            path = /home/user/simpleservice/logs/service.log

    3.  Create a restart helper script **/home/user/bin/restart-simpleservice.sh**
        that contains exactly the five lines shown below, then make that script
        executable for the owner (it does NOT need to be executable for group or
        others).  Note the shebang and the single tab indentation on line 3:

            #!/usr/bin/env bash
            # restart helper for simpleservice
            LOGFILE="/home/user/simpleservice/logs/restart.log"
            	echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') simpleservice restarted" >> "$LOGFILE"
            exit 0

    4.  Produce an evidence file **/home/user/task_outcome.log** whose content
        follows *exactly* the four-line template shown below.  
        • The first line must be the literal text “Task Completed:” followed
          by a single space and the current date-time stamp in the form
          YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS (24-hour clock).  
        • Lines 2-4 must contain the literal texts shown, each followed by
          exactly one space and then “YES”.  
        • No extra blank lines are allowed; a final newline is required:

            Task Completed: 2023-12-31 23:59:59
            Config File Exists: YES
            Log Directory Exists: YES
            Restart Script Executable: YES

       (Obviously the timestamp must reflect the actual completion time, not the
        sample above.)

    5.  Store all files with the exact names and in the exact locations specified.
        File and directory permissions should be left at defaults except that the
        restart script must be owner-executable.

    How the automated test will check your work
    -------------------------------------------
    • It will verify that the directory /home/user/simpleservice/logs exists.  
    • It will checksum **/home/user/etc/simpleservice.conf** to confirm it
      matches verbatim the eight lines shown above (including the ending newline).  
    • It will confirm that **/home/user/bin/restart-simpleservice.sh** is present,
      contains exactly the five prescribed lines, and has the executable bit set
      for the owner (mode 700, 750 or 755 all acceptable).  
    • It will parse **/home/user/task_outcome.log** and check that:  
        – It consists of exactly four non-blank lines.  
        – The timestamp on line 1 matches the required format.  
        – Lines 2, 3 and 4 match the literal text in the template.  

    Estimated command count: 5-10 shell commands should be sufficient
    (mkdir, echo/redirection or cat >file, chmod, etc.).

    Do NOT display or print this task description to the console when you solve
    the ticket; just carry out the necessary terminal actions so the automated
    test can verify success.
truth:
Expected artefacts for verification:

    1. Directory path that must exist:
       /home/user/simpleservice/logs

    2. File /home/user/etc/simpleservice.conf
       Exact contents (including the final newline, total 8 lines):

       [General]
       port = 5500
       mode = production

       [Logging]
       level = info
       path = /home/user/simpleservice/logs/service.log

    3. File /home/user/bin/restart-simpleservice.sh
       Exact contents (including the final newline, total 5 lines):

       #!/usr/bin/env bash
       # restart helper for simpleservice
       LOGFILE="/home/user/simpleservice/logs/restart.log"
       	echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') simpleservice restarted" >> "$LOGFILE"
       exit 0

       The file must have the executable bit set for the owner (stat %a should
       report 700, 750 or 755).

    4. File /home/user/task_outcome.log
       • Must contain four lines, each ending with a newline.  
       • Line 1 regex: ^Task Completed: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$  
       • Line 2 literal: Config File Exists: YES  
       • Line 3 literal: Log Directory Exists: YES  
       • Line 4 literal: Restart Script Executable: YES

    The automated checker will also verify that no additional unexpected files
    are created in /home/user/etc or /home/user/bin.
